Also, I just realized I should check what changed after clicking the N-container green bar at (21, 42). Let me compare the before and after frames.

sections.content:

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking N-container green bar at (21, 42)
# Compare last transition
if last_transition:
    before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    
    # Compare node counts and properties
    print("Before click:")
    for n in before:
        if n['color'] not in ['B']:  # Skip background
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px, ({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
    
    print("\nAfter click:")
    for n in after:
        if n['color'] not in ['B']:  # Skip background
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px, ({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
    
    # Check timer change
    before_timer = [n for n in before if n['color'] == 'G' and min(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) == 63]
    after_timer = [n for n in after if n['color'] == 'G' and min(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) == 63]
    print(f"\nTimer before: {before_timer[0]['pixels'] if before_timer else '?'}")
    print(f"Timer after: {after_timer[0]['pixels'] if after_timer else '?'}")
    
    # Check if any non-timer object changed
    print("\nNon-timer changes:")
    for n_after in after:
        if n_after['color'] == 'G' and min(p[0] for p in n_after['boundary']) == 63:
            continue  # Skip timer
        for n_before in before:
            if n_before['hash'] == n_after['hash'] and n_before['pixels'] != n_after['pixels']:
                print(f"  {n_after['color']}: {n_before['pixels']} -> {n_after['pixels']}")

# Now try clicking Y-container green bar
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 41, 'col': 24}])
print(f"\nAfter Y-container green bar click: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
